milestones.title: Fractal Pattern Generator
milestones.vision: A complete web-based fractal pattern generator with L-system and IFS engines, designer-grade controls, preset library, symmetry/tiling modes, and optimized SVG export for Figma workflows.
success_criteria: User can select a preset fractal, adjust parameters (density, size, thickness, rotation, branching), and see the result update in real-time
User can export an optimized SVG that imports into Figma as editable vector paths under 50KB for typical patterns
User can apply symmetry modes and tiling to create repeating design patterns from any fractal
User can define custom L-system rules and generate novel fractal patterns

key_risks.risk: Real-time preview performance with complex fractals
key_risks.whyItMatters: Users adjust sliders continuously — if regeneration takes >100ms the tool feels broken. Need to validate Canvas rendering perf early.

key_risks.risk: L-system string rewriting memory/time at high iterations
key_risks.whyItMatters: L-system strings grow exponentially. Need iteration limits and possibly Web Workers to avoid UI thread blocking.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: SVG output with defs/use structural optimization stays under 50KB for 5-iteration fractals and imports cleanly into Figma
Canvas rendering of L-system fractals up to 5 iterations updates within 100ms on parameter change
L-system engine caps iteration depth and provides feedback when limits are hit

milestones.verification_uat: Human visual inspection of generated fractals, SVG import into Figma, parameter responsiveness

milestones.title: IFS (Iterated Function System) Engine
milestones.vision: Add a second fractal generation engine based on Iterated Function Systems. IFS fractals (Barnsley Fern, Sierpiński Triangle, etc.) use affine transformations with probabilities to generate point clouds that converge to attractors. The IFS engine renders as a point cloud on canvas and integrates with the existing color system, symmetry, export, and animation features.

At least 5 IFS presets (Barnsley Fern, Sierpiński Triangle, Dragon, Maple Leaf, Spiral)

Color modes work with IFS (single, gradient by transform, per-transform colors)
SVG export works for IFS point clouds
IFS params work with the animation system
Custom IFS editor for user-defined transforms

# S01: L-System Engine + Core Controls + Live Preview

**Built a complete L-system fractal engine with turtle graphics, 10 preset fractals, interactive parameter controls, and real-time Canvas preview — all rendering instantly.**

## What Happened

S01 delivers the core fractal generation pipeline end-to-end. The L-system engine handles string rewriting with a 2M character safety cap. The turtle graphics interpreter converts L-system strings to geometry arrays supporting all standard commands including branching (push/pop) and turn-around. 10 presets cover a wide range of fractal types from space-filling curves (Hilbert, Dragon) to branching structures (Tree, Bush) to classic curves (Koch, Sierpinski, Levy). The control panel provides three sections of sliders (Structure, Appearance) with a 2-column preset grid. The Canvas renderer handles DPR awareness, auto-fit scaling, rotation, and re-renders on every parameter change with no perceptible lag even at 2000+ segments. The UI uses a dark lab-instrument aesthetic with accent glow effects.

## Verification

All 10 presets render correctly with visually distinct shapes. All 6 parameter sliders produce visible real-time changes. Segment counts match expected values (Koch depth 4 = 768, Dragon depth 10 = 1024, Hilbert depth 5 = 2047). Build passes cleanly. No console errors.

## Requirements Advanced

- R001 — L-system engine generates all preset fractals with real-time preview
- R002 — All core parameter controls (iterations, angle, length ratio, scale, stroke, rotation) are interactive sliders
- R004 — 10 preset fractals provided (exceeds 7+ target)
- R011 — Canvas preview updates instantly for all presets up to max iterations

# S02: SVG Export Hardening + IFS Structural Optimization

**Hardened SVG export with defs/use structural optimization for branching fractals, inline preview, timing display, edge case handling, and cross-preset validation**

S02 added three major capabilities to the SVG export pipeline. T01 implemented IFS-style defs/use structural optimization that detects repeated branch patterns in branching fractals (trees, bushes), normalizes their geometry, and emits shared definitions with transform-based placements. This activates automatically when branches have 5+ segments and the savings justify the overhead. T02 added a polished SVG preview toggle, generation timing display with SSR hydration fix, high-segment count warnings, and empty geometry handling. T03 validated all 10 presets export valid SVG in both optimized and expanded modes with 86-95% file size reductions, plus edge case coverage.

## Verification

All 10 presets export valid SVG in both modes. Fractal Bush triggers defs/use optimization. Empty and single-segment geometry handled gracefully. SVG preview renders correctly. No console errors. No hydration mismatches. TypeScript compiles cleanly.

# S03: Color, Style, Jitter + Custom L-System Rules

**Added depth-based color gradient, angle/length jitter with seeded PRNG, and custom L-system rule editor — transforming the tool from preset viewer to creative design instrument**

## What Happened

S03 added three creative capabilities. T01 implemented depth-based color gradients with HSL interpolation, dual color pickers, gradient preview bar, and per-depth coloring in both Canvas renderer and SVG export. T02 added angle and length jitter using a mulberry32 seeded PRNG for deterministic randomness, with percentage-based sliders and a seed randomize button. T03 built a custom L-system rule editor with axiom input, multi-line rule parsing, validation, command reference, and full integration with the rendering pipeline. All features work together — users can now create a custom fractal, apply organic jitter, color it with a gradient, and export a fully-optimized SVG.

Depth gradient renders correctly on Fractal Tree (green→purple). 15% angle jitter produces organic variation. Custom rules (F=G[+F]-F, G=GG) generate valid fractals. All features compose correctly. No console errors. TypeScript compiles cleanly throughout.

tasks.one_liner: Implemented defs/use structural optimization for branching fractals with automatic branch detection, shape normalization, and threshold-based activation
tasks.narrative:
Added branchId tracking to the turtle interpreter — each `[` push creates a unique branch ID. Built `defs-optimizer.ts` that normalizes branches to local coordinates, hashes their shape for comparison, and identifies repeated patterns. The SVG generator now checks if defs/use optimization is beneficial and, when it is, emits `<defs>` with shared branch shapes and `<use>` elements with translate+rotate transforms.

Key design decisions: (1) Only branches with 5+ segments qualify for defs/use — smaller branches have more `<use>` overhead than they save. (2) fitScale is baked into the def path data rather than applied via `<use> scale()` — this avoids stroke-width distortion. (3) Non-branching fractals (Koch, Dragon, Hilbert) fall through to the standard depth-grouped path chaining which is already highly effective (85-87% savings).

For Fractal Tree at depth 7: optimized SVG is 7.9 KB (88% smaller than expanded 66.3 KB), with defs/use providing ~2% additional savings over pure path chaining. For Fractal Bush at depth 5: 48 KB optimized vs 354 KB expanded (86% savings). Visual output verified identical between optimized and expanded modes.

Added branchId tracking to the turtle interpreter — each `[` push creates a unique branch ID. Built `defs-optimizer.ts` that normalizes branches to local coordinates, hashes their shape for comparison, and identifies repeated patterns. The SVG generator now checks if defs/use optimization is beneficial and, when it is, emits `<defs>` with shared branch shapes and `<use>` elements with translate+rotate transforms.

Key design decisions: (1) Only branches with 5+ segments qualify for defs/use — smaller branches have more `<use>` overhead than they save. (2) fitScale is baked into the def path data rather than applied via `<use> scale()` — this avoids stroke-width distortion. (3) Non-branching fractals (Koch, Dragon, Hilbert) fall through to the standard depth-grouped path chaining which is already highly effective (85-87% savings).

For Fractal Tree at depth 7: optimized SVG is 7.9 KB (88% smaller than expanded 66.3 KB), with defs/use providing ~2% additional savings over pure path chaining. For Fractal Bush at depth 5: 48 KB optimized vs 354 KB expanded (86% savings). Visual output verified identical between optimized and expanded modes.

## Verification

TypeScript compiles cleanly. Fractal Tree depth 7 exports correct SVG with defs/use (3 defs, 14 uses). Visual comparison of optimized vs expanded SVGs shows identical rendering. Koch Snowflake (non-branching) correctly skips defs/use and uses standard path chaining. No console errors.

decisions.choice: L-system engine with turtle graphics as primary, IFS-style defs/use for SVG optimization
decisions.rationale: L-systems produce the widest variety of designer-useful patterns as clean vector paths. Turtle graphics maps directly to SVG path commands. IFS affine transforms map to SVG use/defs for exponential file compression. Mandelbrot/escape-time fractals excluded because they produce raster-like pixel grids unsuitable for vector design workflows.

decisions.choice: Canvas 2D for live preview, SVG string building for export
decisions.rationale: Canvas is faster for rendering complex fractals with thousands of line segments (no DOM overhead). SVG string building (not DOM manipulation) allows direct optimization of the output — relative path commands, precision control, defs/use — before the string ever touches a parser. This separation means preview stays fast while export stays optimized.

decisions.decision: SVG optimization strategy for Figma compatibility
decisions.choice: Dual-mode export: optimized (defs/use, grouped attributes, minimal file size) and expanded (individual paths, maximum Figma editability)
decisions.rationale: Optimized mode uses SVG defs/use for repeated sub-patterns, reducing file size by 50-70%. But Figma treats use-referenced elements as grouped instances, limiting individual path editing. Expanded mode outputs every path separately, larger but fully editable in Figma. Users need both depending on whether they want a lightweight asset or a fully decomposed design element.

decisions.choice: Seeded PRNG applied during turtle interpretation, not during L-system string generation
decisions.rationale: Applying jitter at turtle interpretation time (not string rewriting time) means the L-system string can be cached and reused. Same seed always produces the same visual output, enabling reproducible sharing via URL. Jitter at the string level would make strings uncacheable and non-deterministic.
